paper.title: New nearby hypervelocity stars and their spatial distribution from Gaia DR2

review.summary: Paper presents 28 nearby late-type HVSs (P_ub > 0.99) from Gaia DR2 5D tangential velocity selection, plus 28 stars (P_ub > 0.5) from 6D total velocity selection. Contains 3 structured data tables: a classification criteria table (Table 1), a 28-row 5D-unbound HVS catalog (Table 2), and a 28-row 6D sample catalog categorized by origin class (Table 3). No external data resources or machine-readable files are mentioned beyond the in-paper tables. The DUST URL in the source is commented out; generic Gaia/DPAC URLs in acknowledgements are excluded per fidelity rules.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Defines the classification criteria used to categorize HVS candidates by origin based on orbit integration. Stars are classified as HS (Galactic center origin, P_gc > 0.16), OUT (extragalactic origin, P_gc < 0.16 and P_MW < 0.5), or HRS (hyper-runaway star, P_gc < 0.16 and P_MW > 0.5). Used to categorize the 28 stars in Table 3.

source_refs.caption: The probability of stars are used as the classified criteria
source_refs.label: class

columns.name: Class
columns.meaning: Origin classification of the star based on orbit integration probabilities

columns.name: P_gc
columns.meaning: Probability that the star originated from the Galactic center (R_min < 1 kpc), computed from MCMC orbit realizations integrated backward over 5 Gyr

columns.name: P_MW
columns.meaning: Probability that the star originated from within the Milky Way (R_min < 25 kpc), computed from MCMC orbit realizations integrated backward over 5 Gyr

internal_tables.evidence: Section 4: 'For each 6D-unbound HVS, we use the MCMC realizations discussed above and integrate each orbit back in a total time of 5 Gyr. Then we define the probability that a star is derived from the Galactic center P_gc (R_min < 1 kpc) and from the Galaxy P_MW (R_min < 25 kpc). The classified criteria of stars' origin are shown in Table 1.'
internal_tables.comments: Table 1 (label: class). This is a short reference table defining classification thresholds, not a data catalog.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Primary data table listing 28 nearby (d < 6 kpc) late-type hypervelocity stars with unbound probability P_ub > 0.99 identified from Gaia DR2 5D information (parallax + proper motions, no radial velocities). These are the main '5D-unbound' HVS sample used to study spatial anisotropy. Superscripts B, H, M, S mark sources also listed in Bromley18, Hattori18, Marchetti18, and Scholz18 respectively.

source_refs.caption: HVS identified from tangential velocity selection of Gaia DR2 5D information

columns.name: source_id
columns.meaning: Gaia DR2 source identifier

columns.name: l
columns.meaning: Galactic longitude

columns.name: b
columns.meaning: Galactic latitude

columns.name: varpi
columns.meaning: Parallax with uncertainty

columns.name: mu_alpha_star
columns.meaning: Proper motion in right ascension (mu_alpha * cos(delta)) with uncertainty

columns.name: mu_delta
columns.meaning: Proper motion in declination with uncertainty

columns.name: d
columns.meaning: Heliocentric distance derived via Bayesian inference with an exponentially decreasing space density prior, reported with asymmetric uncertainties

columns.name: r_gc
columns.meaning: Projected Galactocentric distance in cylindrical coordinates, computed from coordinate transformations

columns.name: v_gc
columns.meaning: Total velocity in the Galactic rest frame (Galactocentric total tangential velocity corrected for solar motion), reported with asymmetric uncertainties from MCMC

internal_tables.evidence: Section 2.4: 'There are 28 stars with the probability of unbound P_ub > 0.99 (red star) from 5D information... In the appendix, Table 2 provides Gaia identifiers, position, parallaxes, proper motions, distances and the Galaxy's velocity of HVSs with the probability of unbound P_ub > 0.99 from Gaia DR2 5D information'
internal_tables.comments: Table 2 in the paper appendix. Contains 28 rows of HVS data. This is the main 5D sample. Superscripts on source_id entries indicate cross-matches: S=Scholz18 (4 stars), B=Bromley18 (1 star). No radial velocity measurements available for these stars.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Primary data table listing 28 stars with unbound probability P_ub > 0.5 identified from Gaia DR2 6D information (parallax + proper motions + radial velocities). Stars are organized into three origin classes: 3 HS candidates (Galactic center origin), 12 OUT candidates (extragalactic origin), and 13 HRS candidates (hyper-runaway stars from the disk). Includes the unbound probability P_ub for each star. Superscripts B, H, M mark sources also listed in Bromley18, Hattori18, and Marchetti18.

source_refs.caption: HVS identified from total velocity selection of Gaia DR2 6D information

columns.name: source_id
columns.meaning: Gaia DR2 source identifier

columns.name: l
columns.meaning: Galactic longitude

columns.name: b
columns.meaning: Galactic latitude

columns.name: varpi
columns.meaning: Parallax with uncertainty

columns.name: mu_alpha_star
columns.meaning: Proper motion in right ascension (mu_alpha * cos(delta)) with uncertainty

columns.name: mu_delta
columns.meaning: Proper motion in declination with uncertainty

columns.name: rv
columns.meaning: Heliocentric radial velocity with uncertainty, from Gaia DR2 (rv_nb_transits > 5 quality cut applied)

columns.name: d
columns.meaning: Heliocentric distance derived via Bayesian inference with an exponentially decreasing space density prior, reported with asymmetric uncertainties

columns.name: r_gc
columns.meaning: Projected Galactocentric distance in cylindrical coordinates

columns.name: v_gc
columns.meaning: Total velocity in the Galactic rest frame, computed from full 6D phase space information, reported with asymmetric uncertainties from MCMC

columns.name: P_ub
columns.meaning: Probability of being unbound from the Galaxy, computed as the fraction of MCMC realizations (10000 samples) resulting in V_gc >= V_esc at the star's position

internal_tables.evidence: Section 2.4: '28 stars with P_ub > 0.5 (red hexagon) from 6D information... Table 3 provides the same parameters (the last column is the probability of unbound) with P_ub > 0.5 from Gaia DR2 6D information.' Section 4: 'We find 3 hypervelocity stars (HS) that could originate in the Galactic center, 12 stars with extragalactic origin (OUT) and 13 hyper-runaway star (HRS) candidates.'
internal_tables.comments: Table 3 in the paper appendix. Contains 28 rows organized by origin class (HS/OUT/HRS). Superscripts on source_id entries: B=Bromley18 (5 stars), H=Hattori18 (2 stars), M=Marchetti18 (4 stars). Only 3 stars have P_ub near 0.99; the majority are in the 0.5-0.9 range.
